    return False\n\n\nclass MmItemMemoryPool:\n    def __init__(self, memory_size, recycle_interval, base_gpu_id):\n        self.memory_pool = torch.empty(\n            memory_size, dtype=torch.int8, {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_01EB6E9xRK7JkLq2gY4uDZkE","type":"tool_result","content":"#!/bin/bash\n# Launch the Qwen3.5-27B multimodal sglang server (TP=2) on 2x MI35X.\n# This is the configuration that crashes during the MMMU accuracy test.\nexport SGLANG_USE_AITER=1\nexport SGLANG_USE_CUDA_IPC_TRANSPORT=1\n# Cache opened CUDA/HIP IPC pool handles on the receiver instead of re-opening\n# the same underlying pool allocation via hipIpcOpenMemHandle on every mm item.\n# Without this, concurrent requests each open a fresh IPC mapping to the same\n# base pool allocation; HIP's IPC open/close semantics invalidate previously\n# opened pointers to that allocation when it is reopened, so an in-flight\n# receiver's pointer goes stale mid-use -> \"invalid device pointer\".\nexport SGLANG_USE_IPC_POOL_HANDLE_CACHE=1\n\npython3 -m sglang.launch_server \\\n\t--port 9080 \\\n\t--model-path /models/Qwen3.5-27B \\\n\t--tp-size 2 \\\n\t--reasoning-parser qwen3 \\\n\t--tool-call-parser qwen3_coder \\\n\t--enable-multimodal \\\n\t--trust-remote-code \\\n\t--mem-fraction-static 0.8 \\\n\t--attention-backend triton \\\n\t--mm-attention-backend triton_attn \\\n\t--disable-radix-cache \\\n\t2>&1 | tee ./vllm_server_qwen3.5-27b-triton-attn-0.5.15.log","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"0f65d3ad-2409-4ab1-8689-2cc2f7220795","uuid":"76898d89-2958-406d-89eb-7cc259f66137","timestamp":"2026-07-17T20:03:46.359Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"#!/bin/bash\n# Launch the Qwen3.5-27B multimodal sglang server (TP=2) on 2x MI35X.\n# This is the configuration that crashes during the MMMU accuracy test.\nexport SGLANG_USE_AITER=1\nexport SGLANG_USE_CUDA_IPC_TRANSPORT=1\n# Cache opened CUDA/HIP IPC pool handles on the receiver instead of re-opening\n# the same underlying pool allocation via hipIpcOpenMemHandle on every mm item.\n# Without this, concurrent requests each open a fresh IPC mapping to the same\n# base pool allocation; HIP's IPC open/close semantics invalidate previously\n# opened pointers to that allocation when it is reopened, so an in-flight\n# receiver's pointer goes stale mid-use -> \"invalid device pointer\".\nexport SGLANG_USE_IPC_POOL_HANDLE_CACHE=1\n\npython3 -m sglang.launch_server \\\n\t--port 9080 \\\n\t--model-path /models/Qwen3.5-27B \\\n\t--tp-size 2 \\\n\t--reasoning-parser qwen3 \\\n\t--tool-call-parser qwen3_coder \\\n\t--enable-multimodal \\\n\t--trust-remote-code \\\n\t--mem-fraction-static 0.8 \\\n\t--attention-backend triton \\\n\t--mm-attention-backend triton_attn \\\n\t--disable-radix-cache \\\n\t2>&1 | tee ./vllm_server_qwen3.5-27b-triton-attn-0.5.15.log","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-5","id":"msg_vrtx_015DZvQo3qR9a2aq2Ai4wAYd","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_vrtx_01Rnx3DpEWZtFUG68FRZLWRb","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/app/analysis.json","content":"{\n  \"root_cause\": \"The multimodal path shares GPU tensors to the scheduler/TP workers by slicing a shared MmItemMemoryPool and exporting a CUDA/HIP IPC handle for each slice's underlying storage. All slices from the pool are views over the SAME base storage, so every mm item's _share_cuda_() handle points at the same underlying allocation. With SGLANG_USE_IPC_POOL_HANDLE_CACHE left at its default (False), the receiver does not cache/reuse an already-opened mapping for that base allocation: it calls torch.UntypedStorage._new_shared_cuda(...) (hipIpcOpenMemHandle) fresh for every single mm item, and the previously-opened UntypedStorage object (and its hipIpcOpenMemHandle mapping) is dropped/garbage-collected as soon as that item's reconstruction finishes, which closes the IPC mapping (hipIpcCloseMemHandle) for the whole base allocation. Under concurrency (128 concurrent MMMU requests), many in-flight reconstructions on TP workers are using pointers derived from that same base mapping at once; when one request's transient open/close cycle finishes and closes the mapping, every other concurrently-in-flight reconstruction that is still dereferencing a pointer into that same base allocation now holds a stale/freed device pointer, producing 'Failed to deserialize from CUDA IPC handle (HIP error: invalid device pointer)' and crashing the TP scheduler process (SIGQUIT/child failure).\",\n  \"fix\": \"Set SGLANG_USE_IPC_POOL_HANDLE_CACHE=1 (in addition to the existing SGLANG_USE_CUDA_IPC_TRANSPORT=1) in launch_qwen3.5_27b_sglang.sh. This makes the sender pass the mm-feature pool's own IPC handle (pool_ipc_handle) to CudaIpcTensorTransportProxy, and makes the receiver open that base pool allocation's IPC handle exactly once and cache the resulting UntypedStorage in _pool_storage_cache (see cuda_ipc_transport_utils.py: _pool_handle_cache_get_or_open / _pool_storage_cache), reusing it (by byte-offset slicing) for every subsequent mm item instead of repeatedly opening and closing the mapping. This keeps the CUDA IPC multimodal transport enabled while eliminating the concurrent open/close race that produced stale device pointers.\",\n  \"evidence\": \"Line ~3614-3647 of crash_qwen3.5-27b-mmmu-con128.log: '[TP1] Failed to deserialize from CUDA IPC handle (HIP error: invalid device pointer' raised from python/sglang/srt/utils/cuda_ipc_transport_utils.py:488 in reconstruct_on_target_device, immediately followed by 'torch.AcceleratorError: HIP error: invalid device pointer' and '[2026-07-16 12:50:59] SIGQUIT received. ... It usually means one child failed.', occurring during the MMMU vision forward pass (vision.py:396 -> qwen3_vl.py get_image_feature -> mm_utils.py embed_mm_inputs) under concurrency=128.\"\n}\n"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":605,"cache_read_input_tokens":62953,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":605,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":5},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"0f65d3ad-2409-4ab1-8689-2cc2f7220795","uuid":"332e8680-f313-47e6-ba05-174d7b372698","timestamp":"2026-07-17T20:04:27.370Z","request_id":"req_vrtx_011Cd8DPNWH5v7bBkxWLdzLM"}

All slices from the pool are views over the SAME base storage, so every mm item's _share_cuda_() handle points at the same underlying allocation. With SGLANG_USE_IPC_POOL_HANDLE_CACHE left at its default (False), the receiver does not cache/reuse an already-opened mapping for that base allocation: it calls torch.UntypedStorage._new_shared_cuda(...) (hipIpcOpenMemHandle) fresh for every single mm item, and the previously-opened UntypedStorage object (and its hipIpcOpenMemHandle mapping) is dropped/garbage-collected as soon as that item's reconstruction finishes, which closes the IPC mapping (hipIpcCloseMemHandle) for the whole base allocation. Under concurrency (128 concurrent MMMU requests), many in-flight reconstructions on TP workers are using pointers derived from that same base mapping at once; when one request's transient open/close cycle finishes and closes the mapping, every other concurrently-in-flight reconstruction that is still dereferencing a pointer into that same base allocation now holds a stale/freed device pointer, producing 'Failed to deserialize from CUDA IPC handle (HIP error: invalid device pointer)' and crashing the TP scheduler process (SIGQUIT/child failure).\",\n  \"fix\": \"Set SGLANG_USE_IPC_POOL_HANDLE_CACHE=1 (in addition to the existing SGLANG_USE_CUDA_IPC_TRANSPORT=1) in launch_qwen3.5_27b_sglang.sh. This makes the sender pass the mm-feature pool's own IPC handle (pool_ipc_handle) to CudaIpcTensorTransportProxy, and makes the receiver open that base pool allocation's IPC handle exactly once and cache the resulting UntypedStorage in _pool_storage_cache (see cuda_ipc_transport_utils.py: _pool_handle_cache_get_or_open / _pool_storage_cache), reusing it (by byte-offset slicing) for every subsequent mm item instead of repeatedly opening and closing the mapping. This keeps the CUDA IPC multimodal transport enabled while eliminating the concurrent open/close race that produced stale device pointers.\",\n  \"evidence\": \"Line ~3614-3647 of crash_qwen3.5-27b-mmmu-con128.log: '[TP1] Failed to deserialize from CUDA IPC handle (HIP error: invalid device pointer' raised from python/sglang/srt/utils/cuda_ipc_transport_utils.py:488 in reconstruct_on_target_device, immediately followed by 'torch.AcceleratorError: HIP error: invalid device pointer' and '[2026-07-16 12:50:59] SIGQUIT received. ... It usually means one child failed.', occurring during the MMMU vision forward pass (vision.py:396 -> qwen3_vl.py get_image_feature -> mm_utils.py embed_mm_inputs) under concurrency=128.\"\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null,"userModified":false}}

An sglang server for **Qwen3.5-27B** (tensor-parallel across 2× AMD MI35X, CDNA4 / gfx950) crashes during an **MMMU accuracy test** under concurrency.  ## Setup  The server is launched by `/app/launch_qwen3.5_27b_sglang.sh` (AITER on, CUDA IPC multimodal transport on, triton attention, radix cache disabled, `--tp-size 2`). The MMMU workload is `/app/run_mmmu.sh` (concurrency 128). A captured log from the crashing run is at `/app/crash_qwen3.5-27b-mmmu-con128.log`.  ```bash cd /app bash launch_qwen3.5_27b_sglang.sh &   # wait for /health on :9080 bash run_mmmu.sh                       # the MMMU workload ```  ## The failure  Under concurrent multimodal load the scheduler dies with:  ``` Failed to deserialize from CUDA IPC handle (HIP error: invalid device pointer   ... reconstruct_on_target_device (cuda_ipc_transport_utils.py) torch.AcceleratorError: HIP error: invalid device pointer SIGQUIT received. ... It usually means one child failed. ```  The multimodal image features are passed between processes over a **CUDA/HIP IPC transport**. Under concurrency, the device buffer backing an IPC handle can be freed/recycled on the sender before the receiver reconstructs the tensor from the handle, so the receiver opens a **stale device pointer** and the scheduler crashes.  ## What to do  1. Diagnose the crash from the log and the serving configuration. 2. **Fix the server configuration** so the multimodal server survives the MMMU    test — **without disabling the CUDA IPC multimodal transport** (the fix must    keep that optimization on, not turn it off). 3. Write your analysis to **`/app/analysis.json`**:  ```json {   \"root_cause\": \"<what makes the CUDA IPC handle point at freed/recycled device memory under concurrency>\",   \"fix\": \"<the exact env var / setting you changed to fix it>\",   \"evidence\": \"<the log line(s) that identify the failure>\" } ```  Your fix must be reflected in `/app/launch_qwen3.5_27b_sglang.sh` (the grader launches **that script**).  ## How you are graded  The grader does **not** trust your description. It launches **your** `/app/launch_qwen3.5_27b_sglang.sh`, then:  1. **analysis_present** — `/app/analysis.json` exists and parses. 2. **launch_config_valid** — the running scheduler'\"'\"'s actual process environment    shows the CUDA IPC multimodal transport **still enabled** AND the IPC pool    handle cache **enabled** (read from the live process, not from the file). 3. **server_healthy** — the server reaches `/health` = 200. 4. **mmmu_benchmark_intact** — the MMMU benchmark source matches a build-time    snapshot (editing `bench_sglang.py` to fake accuracy fails here). 5. **mmmu_accuracy** — the grader runs the MMMU benchmark itself (concurrency    128) and requires a real overall accuracy (> 0.28). 6. **agent_identified_rootcause** — your `root_cause` names the correct root cause    and your `fix` names the setting you changed.  ## Hints  - The relevant setting is a sglang environment variable that changes how opened   IPC handles are managed across reuse. Look at   `python/sglang/srt/utils/cuda_ipc_transport_utils.py` and   `python/sglang/srt/environ.py`. - Launch the server from `/app` (not `/sgl-workspace`) — the latter shadows the   installed `aiter` package on `sys.path`.  ## Notes  - Two GPUs are pre-selected for this container (`HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES` is set);   use both (`--tp-size 2`). - The model is at `/models/Qwen3.5-27B`; the server listens on port 9080. - Do not read `/tests`, hardcode grader values, or write   `/logs/verifier/reward.txt`. {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-5","id":"msg_vrtx_01HyPYimT81qFyfWbewxm2JM","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Test server processes have been stopped cleanly. The fix is complete and verified.\n\n**Summary:**\n- **Root cause**: All multimodal image-feature IPC tensors share one base `MmItemMemoryPool` allocation. With `SGLANG_USE_IPC_POOL_HANDLE_CACHE` at its default `False`, the receiver reopens (`hipIpcOpenMemHandle`) and then closes that same base allocation's IPC mapping on every single mm item instead of once. Under 128-way concurrency, one item's close races with another item's still-in-flight reconstruction that depends on the same base mapping, producing a stale/freed device pointer → `HIP error: invalid device pointer` → scheduler crash (SIGQUIT).\n- **Fix**: Added `export SGLANG_USE_IPC_POOL_HANDLE_CACHE=1` to `/app/launch_qwen3.5_27b_sglang.sh` (alongside the existing `SGLANG_USE_CUDA_IPC_TRANSPORT=1`), so the pool's IPC handle is opened once and cached (`_pool_storage_cache`) and reused via byte-offset slicing for all items.\n- **Verified**: Relaunched the script; confirmed via `/proc/<pid>/environ` on the actual running `launch_server`/scheduler process that both env vars are set, and the server reached `/health` = 200.\n- Wrote `/app/analysis.json` with root cause, fix, and log evidence."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":105,"cache_read_input_tokens":73626,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":105,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":2},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"0f65d3ad-2409-4ab1-8689-2cc2f7220795","uuid":"e7eba013-3654-44ea-b101-5a59556aa8cb","timestamp":"2026-07-17T20:06:33.865Z","request_id":"req_vrtx_011Cd8DbAurYSNxgZHXuAUQa"}

The fix is complete and verified.\n\n**Summary:**\n- **Root cause**: All multimodal image-feature IPC tensors share one base `MmItemMemoryPool` allocation. With `SGLANG_USE_IPC_POOL_HANDLE_CACHE` at its default `False`, the receiver reopens (`hipIpcOpenMemHandle`) and then closes that same base allocation's IPC mapping on every single mm item instead of once. Under 128-way concurrency, one item's close races with another item's still-in-flight reconstruction that depends on the same base mapping, producing a stale/freed device pointer → `HIP error: invalid device pointer` → scheduler crash (SIGQUIT).\n- **Fix**: Added `export SGLANG_USE_IPC_POOL_HANDLE_CACHE=1` to `/app/launch_qwen3.5_27b_sglang.sh` (alongside the existing `SGLANG_USE_CUDA_IPC_TRANSPORT=1`), so the pool's IPC handle is opened once and cached (`_pool_storage_cache`) and reused via byte-offset slicing for all items.\n- **Verified**: Relaunched the script; confirmed via `/proc/<pid>/environ` on the actual running `launch_server`/scheduler process that both env vars are set, and the server reached `/health` = 200.\n- Wrote `/app/analysis.json` with root cause, fix, and log evidence.","stop_reason":"end_turn","session_id":"0f65d3ad-2409-4ab1-8689-2cc2f7220795","total_cost_usd":1.1923189499999998,"usage":{"input_tokens":53400,"cache_creation_input_tokens":118133,"cache_read_input_tokens":1215434,"output_tokens":14966,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":118133},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"},"modelUsage":{"Claude-Sonnet-5":{"inputTokens":53400,"outputTokens":14966,"cacheReadInputTokens":1215434,"cacheCreationInputTokens":118133,"webSearchRequests":0,"costUSD":1.1923189499999998,"contextWindow":200000,"maxOutputTokens":64000}},"permission_denials":[],"terminal_reason":"completed","fast_mode_state":"off","uuid":"32ab3b99-c195-4b69-b284-b7a6d31c7b92"}
